plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for broad index and income focus.
Buy ANET at 10% as a high-quality scanner setup.
Hold a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation.
This plan significantly de-risks the portfolio.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% monthly return, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but potential upside is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL completely and trim SPAL to 5% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a performing tech name.
Increase QQQI to 22% for balanced income exposure.
Buy NOW at 8% as a top-scoring scanner setup.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5% monthly growth, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index, quality tech, and a small allocation to leveraged ETFs. Moderate drawdowns are possible, but within a managed risk framework.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Add to SOXL up to 10% for aggressive leveraged semiconductor exposure.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press into a strong performer.
Increase SMH to 15% for broader semiconductor exposure.
Buy TECL at 10% and ARM at 10% as high-scoring leveraged and tech setups.
Maintain a 5% cash buffer, using leverage to amplify returns.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by leaning into leverage and high-scoring setups. It carries significantly higher risk, with larger potential drawdowns and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the amplified risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and free up capital.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single-name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize income generation.
Increase QQQ to 20% for broad index stability alongside income.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer for stability and future income opportunities.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly distributions, prioritizing steady yield and low drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta single names, focusing on income-generating ETFs and broad index exposure. While aiming for stability, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in market downturns.
